mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance on modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments where low latency and high throughput are critical, particularly for large language models. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary for your requirements.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Installs llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies autoscaling configurations.
> Sets up monitoring for llm-d.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d release.
> Deletes the llm-d namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step upgrades llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backs up current llm-d configurations.
> Upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verifies that the llm-d pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or the registry is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the registry is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the service status and ensure it is correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators, improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
